posts.title: Best CI/CD for mobile apps in 2026: a practical comparison
posts.description: Comparing EAS Workflows, Bitrise, Codemagic, GitHub Actions, and others on build speed, pricing, and what actually matters for mobile teams.
posts.author: Jon Samp

posts.title: Faster iOS Builds with Precompiled XCFrameworks
posts.description: Starting with SDK 56, Expo packages on iOS ship as prebuilt XCFrameworks, so clean iOS builds skip compiling 70+ packages from source.
posts.author: Christian Falch

posts.title: How a Kotlin compiler plugin cut Android time to first render by 30%
posts.description: A new Kotlin compiler plugin in SDK 56 strips reflection from Expo Modules on Android: 70% faster init, no code changes for app developers.
posts.author: Lukasz Kosmaty

posts.title: Moving away from @expo/vector-icons
posts.description: We cut our repro app's bundle by 4 MB by switching from @expo/vector-icons to @react-native-vector-icons. Here's why we now recommend the change.
posts.author: Vojtech Novak

posts.title: What happened at AppJS 2026? Highlights, new products, and plans for the future
posts.description: A recap of the Expo talks from App.js 2026, including a full year of releases and Expo Observe, our new production performance library now in public beta.
posts.author: Jon Samp

posts.title: Talking to JSI in Swift: what changed in SDK 56
posts.description: In SDK 56, Expo's native modules call JSI directly on Apple platforms. The Objective-C++ layer is gone, and calls are 1.6-2.3x faster.
posts.author: Tomasz Sapeta

posts.title: Native code in Expo SDK 56: inline modules and type generation
posts.description: Write Swift and Kotlin modules right next to your app files, then let SDK 56 generate the matching TypeScript interfaces automatically.
posts.author: Hubert Bernacki

posts.title: Expo Router v56: Decoupling from React Navigation
posts.description: Expo Router v56 forks from React Navigation, ships streaming SSR, and closes the platform gap with a new Android toolbar and Native Tabs updates.
posts.author: Jakub Tkacz

posts.title: Worklet integration in Expo UI: synchronously controlling SwiftUI and Compose state
posts.description: Expo UI now integrates with react-native-worklets, letting you control SwiftUI and Compose state synchronously on the UI thread. No JS round-trips.
posts.author: Nishan Bende

posts.title: Expo UI is now stable: SwiftUI and Jetpack Compose from a single import
posts.description: Starting with SDK 56, @expo/ui is stable. One import gives you SwiftUI on iOS and Jetpack Compose on Android. No JS reimplementation.
posts.author: Kudo Chien

posts.title: How to upgrade to Expo SDK 56
posts.description: How to upgrade to Expo SDK 56: step-by-step tips, New Architecture migration advice, breaking changes, and troubleshooting for a smooth update.
posts.author: Keith Kurak

posts.title: Best way to build a mobile app that makes money in 2026
posts.description: An opinionated take on the best Expo stack, what data tells about app monetization, and why you should still choose React Native to win.

posts.title: ASO Skills: ASO in Your AI Workflow

posts.title: From React web to Native in one week

posts.title: Build fast, no matter what: how Expo is optimizing for speed (and how you can, too)

posts.description: How CineMe shipped an AI selfie-to-video app to iOS and Android in just over a month, using Expo and EAS Build to skip platform divergence.

posts.description: Tendbble built a real-time video social app with two frontend devs. Here's how they solved memory, 60fps animation, and camera lifecycle on both platforms.

posts.title: From Istanbul to #1 in the US App Store: why FERASET bet its AI app studio on Expo
posts.description: FERASET shipped their first AI app to both stores in 4 weeks and hit #1 globally, beating Spotify. Here's why this Istanbul studio bet everything on Expo.
